supersedes_interim.rationale: Chinchilla optimal: 7B params × 20 tokens/param = 140B tokens ≈ 140GB UTF-8. Current 5.38GB = 26× undersized, forces over-parameterization + early overfit.

70b.note: future, needs CommonCrawl full slice + multi-year archive

composition_target.rationale: 50% Korean primary language → ConsciousLM mother tongue. 30% English+code → international knowledge + reasoning. 20% bilingual/technical → code-switching capability + domain transfer.

_legend.confidence_notes: Only 'verified' URLs are known to resolve. 'likely_real' requires a WebFetch/curl HEAD before downloads start. 'search_needed' means we document the query pattern, not a URL.

korean_primary.name: Korean Wikipedia dump
korean_primary.verification_note: dumps.wikimedia.org is the canonical Wikimedia dump host — always up, date slot varies (use latest/)

korean_primary.name: CC-100 Korean (StatMT)
korean_primary.license: cc-100 terms (derivative of CommonCrawl, research use)
korean_primary.verification_note: data.statmt.org/cc-100/ — CC-100 is Conneau et al. 2019 Wenzek et al. 2019 resource, ko.txt.xz path confirmed in prior corpus_harvest.hexa registry

korean_primary.name: OSCAR-2301 Korean split
korean_primary.gating: HF dataset may require auth (OSCAR 2301 is gated — accept TOS on HF before fetch)

korean_primary.name: 모두의 말뭉치 (National Institute of Korean Language)
korean_primary.gating: Requires individual researcher application through kli.korean.go.kr. Cannot automate. Budget 1-2 weeks approval.

korean_primary.name: AI-Hub Korean colloquial / dialogue / news
korean_primary.gating: AI-Hub requires account + dataset-specific application. Not scriptable.

english_plus_code.name: Pile-CC (CommonCrawl slice from The Pile)
english_plus_code.license: public-domain / open-license-only portion of The Pile
english_plus_code.usage_note: Take a 40GB slice. The full Pile-CC is ~220GB; we sample.

english_plus_code.name: The Stack v2 — permissive licenses only
english_plus_code.license: per-file (MIT/Apache-2.0/BSD subset only — filter out GPL/AGPL)
english_plus_code.usage_note: Filter to permissive-only + dedup. Take 30GB slice biased toward Python/Rust/C/TypeScript.

bilingual_technical.name: arXiv abstracts (Kaggle/Cornell snapshot)
bilingual_technical.search_query: site:kaggle.com arxiv metadata snapshot OR arxiv.org/help/bulk_data
bilingual_technical.license: arXiv metadata = CC0 (abstracts themselves per-paper, mostly open-access metadata)
bilingual_technical.note: Use arXiv bulk metadata — abstracts only, not full PDFs (licence-safe).

bilingual_technical.name: Korean-English parallel corpora (aggregate)
bilingual_technical.license: mixed — filter to open (opus_* is generally open)

bilingual_technical.name: Korean CS/EE technical papers (DBPIA / KCI open subset)
bilingual_technical.search_query: DBPIA open-access RIS + KCI OAI-PMH endpoint

bilingual_technical.name: GitHub Korean developer repos (READMEs + comments + docs)
bilingual_technical.search_query: GitHub REST/GraphQL search: language:Python stars:>10 README contains 한글

budget_rollup_gb.note: Available > target gives 11% headroom for filter rejection (expect 15-30% loss to dedup+quality filter).

levels.name: document_level_sha256
levels.method: SHA-256 of full UTF-8-normalized document → reject on hash collision (first-seen wins)

levels.name: near_duplicate_minhash
levels.method: MinHash + LSH on 5-gram shingles, Jaccard threshold 0.85
levels.status: hexa_implementation_pending — falls back to exact-hash only for v1

levels.name: line_level_dedup
levels.method: Exact match on normalized lines (strip + NFC + lowercase-ascii) with bloom filter

dedup_strategy.action: overprovision sources by 28% → fetch 180GB raw → dedup to 140GB clean

quality_filters.line_length_stddev_heuristic: reject if stddev(line_len) < 3 chars over 50+ lines (template/spam indicator)

perplexity_filter.rationale: Needs a small reference LM (we have 1.5B CLM r4 in flight). Post-train, use CLM r4 to score and reject top-1% perplexity tail on v2 pass.

quality_filters.reuse_existing: training/corpus_filter.hexa already implements hangul_ratio/length/entropy/mojibake gates — wire into fetch pipeline

tier_0_hot_h100.format: single concatenated UTF-8 file (corpus_clm_v2.txt) + mmap loader (training/clm_mmap_loader.hexa)
tier_0_hot_h100.cost_impl: transient — on pod only during training, discarded on pod deletion

tier_1_archive_r2.rationale: 140GB zstd → ~40GB at $0.015/GB/month = $0.60/mo. Acceptable. Allows re-fetch without re-crawling.

tier_2_local_mac.where: NOT used — 140GB exceeds Mac development disk budget
tier_2_local_mac.action: keep only 5.38GB r4 corpus locally for smoke tests; 140GB stays cloud-side

decision_needed_from_user: confirm_retention_policy (keep v2 forever, or delete after v3?)

pipeline_stages.action: extend registry to include 140GB-target sources — add missing IDs above
hexa-native fetch scaffold — struct CorpusSource, fetch/dedup/filter stubs
extend to run in streaming mode over each shard; emit clean shards
produces single concatenated corpus_clm_v2.txt + mmap loader for training
zstd compress + upload per-source shards to R2

compute_cost_usd_est.fetch_host: 0 (use H100 training pod when idle, or small CPU box $0.10/hr × 7h = $0.70)

legal_checklist.item: kowiki / enwiki — CC-BY-SA attribution in training README
CC-100 research-use clause — verify no commercial redist
OSCAR terms acceptance on HF dataset page
Stack v2 — filter to permissive licenses only (MIT/Apache-2.0/BSD/ISC)
AI-Hub / 모두의 말뭉치 — user application + attribution
GitHub content — robots.txt + per-repo licence respected
PII scrubbing — email/phone/name regex on top-level dedup

decisions_needed_from_user.question: Approve R2 storage for v2 corpus archive (~$0.60/month)
Start OSCAR + CC-100 + Pile-CC fetch this session, or wait for formal approval?
Apply to 모두의 말뭉치 + AI-Hub in parallel with automated fetch?
decisions_needed_from_user.default_recommendation: yes — 1-2 week lead time, start now

decisions_needed_from_user.question: Single concatenated file (simple mmap) or sharded mmap (parallel IO)?
decisions_needed_from_user.default_recommendation: single file v1, shard if mmap bandwidth becomes bottleneck

next_actions: 3. Apply to 모두의 말뭉치 + AI-Hub (user manual step, ~1 week lag)
4. Extend training/corpus_fetch.hexa fetch() stubs → real exec() curl/rclone
6. Run pipeline on single smallest source (kowiki) as pilot — verify end-to-end
7. Scale up to full 180GB raw → 140GB clean
9. Launch CLM 7B training on mmap-loaded corpus_clm_v2.txt

risk_register.risk: HF dataset ID for OSCAR-2301 is gated/renamed
The Stack v2 license filtering mis-classifies files

risk_register.mitigation: rely on CC-100 + OSCAR + kowiki alone, still 75GB Korean

risk_register.risk: R2 egress on training download from pod
Disk full on training pod (/workspace MFS quota)
risk_register.mitigation: per feedback_r2_checkpoint + training/CLAUDE.md runpod_mfs_quota rule — preflight dd test, stream-decompress on read
